titleStmt.title: Letter from George F. Montgomery to the Under-Secretary, 20 June 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: George Fleming Montgomery

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: A letter from George Fleming Montgomery (b. 1867), a Longford solicitor, to Sir Robert Chalmers (1858-1938), Under Secretary for Ireland, applying for the vacant post of Clerk of Crown and Peace for County Westmeath. He outlines his twenty-five year career in the legal profession.Sir Robert Chalmers (1858 - 1938) was sent to Ireland as replacement under-secretary for Sir Matthew Nathan, who had resigned along with Augustine Birrell, his chief secretary, following the outbreak of the Easter Rising in 1916.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/5615/10439

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
GEO F MONTGOMERY SOLICITOR Commissioner For Affidavits Office Every Monday at Granard Longford, 20th June 1916 The Under Secretary The Castle Dublin Sir I beg to apply for the vacant
office of Clerk of Crown & Peace for
the County of Westmeath I am
a practising Solr for the last 25
years having been admitted in
1891 and am now aged 49
years and if appointed I shall
by strict adherence to my duty to
merit the approval of those who
are my superiors I am Sir
Yours faithfully George F Montgomery
